findings.fix: Implement tiered age verification: (1) Lightweight: ID document verification (Veriff, Socure, Jumio) for users claiming 16+; (2) Parental consent: For users unable to verify age, require parental email consent with one-click confirmation; (3) Access restrictions: Block account if age verification fails; (4) Documentation: Add 'Age Verification & Parental Consent Policy' to privacy policy. Start with self-attestation + parental consent for non-verified users.

findings.title: No Age-Appropriate Design Review Documentation
findings.regulation: UK Online Safety Act, California AADC, European Digital Services Act
findings.description: Privacy policy and documentation do not mention age-appropriate design principles (AADC, EDSA, OSA compliance). No evidence of: (1) dark pattern avoidance; (2) nudging transparency; (3) data minimization for minors; (4) parental controls; (5) child safety by design audit. Required by California AADC and UK Online Safety Act if accessed by minors.
findings.fix: Conduct age-appropriate design review: (1) Audit UI for dark patterns (urgency, social proof, infinite scroll); (2) Ensure data minimization (collect minimum necessary); (3) Add parental controls (access restrictions, content filtering); (4) Document design principles in privacy policy & AADC compliance section; (5) Conduct user testing with teens 13-16; (6) Consider hiring AADC consultant (e.g., Common Sense Media). Implement findings.

findings.title: No Data Minimization Policy for Minor Users
findings.description: If minors access the app (despite 16+ policy), no special data protection applies. Privacy policy lacks: (1) special protection for minor user data; (2) shorter retention periods for minors; (3) parental access to child data; (4) restrictions on data sharing with third parties (Anthropic AI, Bolls.life). GDPR Article 8 and COPPA require enhanced protection for child data.
findings.fix: Add privacy policy section: 'Protection of Minor Data': (1) If we discover user is under 16, account is suspended pending parental consent; (2) Minor data is deleted after 30 days if no parental consent provided; (3) Parent can request access to minor's data; (4) Parent can delete child's account; (5) Minimal third-party sharing for minors (e.g., Anthropic API calls anonymized); (6) Shorter retention (30 days vs 90 days for adults). Implement backend enforcement.

findings.title: No Geographic-Aware Age Verification or Enforcement
findings.regulation: UK Online Safety Act, California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA)
findings.description: App does not detect or enforce different age/privacy requirements by geography. UK OSA may require different protections for UK users <18. California CCPA/AADC may apply to California residents. EU GDPR Chapter III applies to EU users. No IP-based geolocation or self-reported location to trigger region-specific rules.
findings.fix: Implement geographic-aware enforcement: (1) Detect user location via IP geolocation (GeoIP2, MaxMind) at signup; (2) Store location in user profile; (3) Apply region-specific rules: UK users <18 require additional consent; CA residents get CCPA deletion rights; EU users get GDPR rights; (4) Display localized privacy policies; (5) Document geo-enforcement in privacy policy.

findings.title: Missing Consent Withdrawal Mechanism
findings.description: Privacy policy does not document how users can withdraw consent for analytics (Plausible) or other consent-based processing. Users should have easy one-click withdrawal without account deletion.
findings.fix: Add account settings section: 'Privacy Preferences': (1) Checkbox to opt-out of Plausible analytics; (2) Checkbox to opt-out of marketing emails; (3) Save preference to user profile; (4) Update privacy policy with withdrawal instructions.

findings.title: No Documented API Security Policy or Standards
findings.description: API lacks documented security standards: (1) no documented authentication method in API spec; (2) no token expiry explanation; (3) no rate-limit documentation; (4) no error handling guidance (e.g., avoid exposing stack traces). Makes it difficult for developers to follow security practices.
findings.fix: Create API Security Policy document: (1) Authentication (Bearer token, expiry 30 days); (2) Rate limits (per endpoint); (3) HTTPS requirement; (4) Error handling (no stack traces, generic 500 errors); (5) Logging (what gets logged, retention); (6) Data minimization (required fields only). Add to API documentation (README, OpenAPI spec).

findings.title: No data retention policy for personal data
findings.description: The privacy policy states operational logs are retained for about 30 days but provides no explicit retention periods for account data. GDPR requires data is kept only as long as necessary. Without documented retention policies, users cannot understand data storage duration.
findings.evidence: Privacy policy: 'Operational logs generally retained about 30 days unless needed longer for security review.' No TTL for user account data, readings, journals.
findings.fix: 1. Document explicit retention periods per data category. 2. Update privacy policy. 3. Implement automated deletion (DynamoDB TTL, scheduled Lambda). 4. Add user-configurable retention settings.

findings.title: Incomplete privacy policy for third-party data processing
findings.description: Privacy discloses Anthropic processes reading context but lacks detail on legal basis, DPA status, data transfers, or user control. Plausible analytics mentioned but no granular consent mechanism.
findings.evidence: Privacy: 'reading context may be processed by Anthropic.' Missing: DPA status, transfer mechanisms, Anthropic's role, user control.
findings.fix: 1. Detail Anthropic as Data Processor with DPA link. 2. Clarify lawful basis and feature necessity. 3. Document data transfers/adequacy decisions. 4. Add explicit analytics consent (opt-in). 5. Link to Anthropic privacy policy.

findings.title: No privacy policy re-consent on updates
findings.description: Policy version tracked but no mechanism requires re-consent if policy updates. Users continue using service indefinitely after policy changes without re-accepting.
findings.evidence: Registration stores privacyPolicyVersion but login doesn't check current version. No forced re-acceptance on policy updates.
findings.fix: 1. Check policy version on login; flag as 'policy_update_pending' if older. 2. Show modal requiring re-acceptance before features. 3. Track re-consent timestamp/version. 4. Implement granular consent management. 5. Document policy versioning.

findings.title: Missing consent for non-essential analytics
findings.description: Plausible analytics script loads unconditionally without consent banner. While cookieless, GDPR requires prior explicit consent for non-essential processing.
findings.evidence: Script loads unconditionally: <script async src='https://analytics.codyjo.com/js/pa-RUx1crBjD6Rrg5MResyha.js' />
findings.fix: 1. Implement consent banner. 2. Separate essential vs. non-essential consent. 3. Load Plausible only after opt-in. 4. Store consent in localStorage. 5. Use opt-in model before analytics.

findings.title: Account deletion doesn't fully purge shared reading tokens
findings.description: Account deletion deletes USER# records but not SHARE# tokens. Shared reading metadata exists with 90-day TTL but incomplete erasure violates right to be forgotten.
findings.evidence: handleDeleteAccount deletes USER# items but not SHARE# tokens. Shares created with 90-day TTL; not cleaned if reading deleted.
findings.fix: 1. Query SHARE# tokens by userId and delete explicitly. 2. Delete SHARE# when reading deleted. 3. Document TTL as secondary mechanism. 4. Confirm all data deleted message.

findings.title: Limited ability to rectify inaccurate data
findings.description: Users can update profile but cannot correct reading/journal data after creation. No audit trail of corrections.
findings.evidence: Settings allows name/email/password changes; readings/journals immutable after creation.
findings.fix: 1. Allow edit of readings (question, notes), journals, daily cards. 2. Maintain immutable audit log. 3. Provide correction log in export. 4. Update privacy policy on data corrections.

findings.title: No opt-out mechanism for marketing/profiling
findings.description: No documented process for objecting to direct marketing or profiling. Privacy mentions 'right to object' but no mechanism.
findings.evidence: Privacy policy mentions right to object but no mechanism or email provided.
findings.fix: 1. Document objection contact (cody@codyjo.com). 2. Add unsubscribe in marketing emails. 3. Implement /api/preferences. 4. Document objection process.

findings.title: Privacy policy doesn't disclose shared reading link expiry
findings.description: App allows 90-day-expiring shared reading links but privacy policy doesn't mention feature, expiry, or public accessibility.
findings.evidence: No mention of sharing feature, 90-day expiry, or public link accessibility.
findings.fix: 1. Add sharing section to privacy policy. 2. Warn users before share generation. 3. Allow immediate revocation instead of waiting for TTL.

findings.title: CSP allows unsafe-inline for scripts
findings.description: CSP includes 'unsafe-inline' for script-src, weakening XSS protection.
findings.evidence: CSP: script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://analytics.codyjo.com
findings.fix: 1. Remove 'unsafe-inline'. 2. Use nonce-based CSP. 3. Restrict to external domains only. 4. Test app functionality.

findings.title: Admin allowlist cached 5 minutes; access retained after removal
findings.description: Admin allowlist cached in Lambda for 5 minutes. Removed admins retain access during cache window.
findings.evidence: ADMIN_CACHE_TTL = 5 * 60 * 1000. Removed admin can access for up to 5 minutes.
findings.fix: 1. Reduce cache to 30-60 seconds. 2. Implement cache invalidation on secret update. 3. Log revocations. 4. Document known risk.

findings.title: 6-digit password reset code vulnerable to brute force
findings.description: Password reset codes use only 6 digits (100000-999999), ~900k values. Rate limit is per-token but token space small.
findings.evidence: generateCode() returns 6-digit number. Reset limited 5 attempts/token but entropy low.
findings.fix: 1. Use 8+ digits or alphanumeric (16+ chars). 2. Implement per-email rate limiting. 3. Add exponential backoff. 4. Consider TOTP or magic links.

findings.title: Admin actions not logged to immutable audit trail
findings.description: Admin actions logged via console.log to ephemeral CloudWatch without immutability guarantee.
findings.evidence: console.log JSON to CloudWatch. No documented retention or immutability.
findings.fix: 1. Send logs to immutable store (S3 versioning, audit service). 2. Include actor, action, target, timestamp, IP, result. 3. Set retention 1+ year. 4. Document access controls. 5. Use SIEM for alerting.

findings.title: No session invalidation or logout token revocation
findings.description: JWT tokens valid for 30 days with no immediate revocation on logout. Stolen tokens usable until expiry.
findings.evidence: Tokens expire after 30 days. No logout revocation mechanism.
findings.fix: 1. Implement token blacklist (DynamoDB). 2. Add JTI (JWT ID) claim. 3. Blacklist on logout. 4. Reduce expiry to 7-14 days.

findings.title: No documented incident response plan
findings.description: No incident response runbook or breach notification procedure documented.
findings.evidence: No incident response plan in codebase or CLAUDE.md.
findings.fix: 1. Create incident response runbook. 2. Define roles/escalation. 3. Document 72-hour breach process. 4. Schedule tabletop exercises. 5. Maintain contact list.

findings.title: Email regex validation could be stricter
findings.description: Simple regex allows invalid formats, rejects valid ones (e.g., '+' character).
findings.fix: 1. Use RFC 5322 validation. 2. Allow '+' in email. 3. Consider third-party validation. 4. Test common formats.
